# Test fixtures

The Rust parity harness reads fixtures directly from `../../t/tdata/` — the
same directory used by the Perl test suite (`t/*.t` via `chdir("t/tdata")`).
This avoids duplicating ~5.7 MB of `.bcf`/`.bib`/`.bbl` files and the drift
that would follow.

## Layout

- `t/tdata/*.bcf` — 53 control files (pipeline inputs).
- `t/tdata/*.bib` — 50 BibTeX datasources.
- `t/tdata/*.bltxml` — biblatexml datasources (handled by `biber-input-biblatexml`).
- `t/tdata/full-bbl.bbl` — the only committed `.bbl` (used by `t/full-bbl.t`).
  Other `.bbl`/`.blg` outputs are gitignored (see `.gitignore`) and are
  regenerated by running `bin/biber` at test time.
- `t/tdata/biber-test.conf`, `tool-test*.conf` — config files used by the
  in-process Perl unit tests.

## Parity strategy

`tests/parity.rs` does **not** rely on checked-in `.bbl` fixtures (only one
is committed). Instead it runs `bin/biber` (Perl) once per `.bcf` to capture
the reference output, then asserts the Rust port matches byte-for-byte. This
mirrors `t/full-bbl.t` but cross-language.

If Perl is unavailable (e.g. in a pure-Rust CI lane), the harness skips with
a clear message rather than failing.

## Adding fixtures

New fixtures belong in `t/tdata/` alongside the Perl tests so both suites
pick them up automatically. Do not add Rust-only fixtures here.
